verdict.answer_first: A: sentence 1 gives the action, "Look at the actual SQL the endpoint runs"; B opens with framing, "2M rows is small".
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to one path: "Find the slow query first" then "Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE"; B surveys causes in a table and ends by asking "What's the database".
verdict.fabrication: B invents unsupported numbers: "single-digit milliseconds", "30-second check", "80% of the time". A has a softer unverified example: "At 50k rows".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute: "slow query logging", "pg_stat_statements", "EXPLAIN ANALYZE", then inspect "Seq Scan" and index coverage.
